split_topic: Whether hacked driver’s license numbers posted online create Article III standing
side_a_holding: Dark-web posting of license numbers established concrete injury.
side_b_holding: Dark-web posting of license numbers did not establish standing.
source: CRS 2025 report, Table 1, Civil Procedure entry
notes: Do not frame as mere acquisition of data; split concerns posted dark-web information.

split_topic: Whether EEOC hyperlink notice starts the 90-day right-to-sue period
side_a_holding: Hyperlink notice was inadequate without clear 90-day warning.
side_b_holding: Hyperlink notice can adequately start the right-to-sue period.
source: CRS 2025 report, Table 1, Civil Rights entry
notes: Good yes/no split on electronic EEOC notice adequacy.

split_topic: Whether Puerto Rico carjacking qualifies as an ACCA violent felony
side_a_holding: Puerto Rico carjacking was not an ACCA violent felony.
side_b_holding: Comparable taking offenses may qualify as ACCA violent felonies.
source: CRS 2025 report, Table 1, Criminal Law & Procedure entry
notes: Side B also includes Tenth Circuit; Seventh chosen as recent representative.

split_topic: Whether Bruen applies to firearm exclusions on state-owned property
side_a_case: Koons v. Att’y Gen. of New Jersey
side_a_holding: State-property firearm exclusions require Bruen historical analysis.
side_b_holding: States may exclude firearms from property like private owners.
notes: Cert granted in part

split_topic: Whether alienage requires clear, unequivocal, and convincing evidence
side_a_holding: Alienage requires clear, unequivocal, and convincing evidence.
side_b_holding: No distinct standard beyond clear and convincing evidence.
notes: Clean standard-of-proof split, but BIA context may be technical.

split_topic: What standard governs notice in FLSA/ADEA collective actions
side_a_holding: Notice requires some similarity evidence and defendant rebuttal opportunity.
side_b_case: Swales v. KLLM Transp. Servs., LLC
side_b_holding: Courts must rigorously assess similarity before issuing notice.
source: CRS 2025 report, Table 1, Labor & Employment entry
notes: Side B also includes Sixth Circuit; Fifth chosen as anchor case.

split_topic: Whether retaliatory hostile work environment requires severe or pervasive harassment
side_a_case: Ogbonna-McGruder v. Austin Peay State Univ.
side_a_holding: Retaliatory hostile environment requires severe or pervasive harassment.
side_b_case: Tonkyro v. Sec’y, Dep’t of Veterans Aff.
side_b_holding: Retaliatory harassment need only deter a reasonable employee.
notes: Clean standard split under Title VII retaliation.

split_topic: Whether Rule 60(b)(4) void-judgment motions have a reasonable-time limit
side_a_case: Burton v. Coney Island Auto Parts Unlimited, Inc.
side_a_holding: Rule 60(b)(4) motions may be denied as untimely.
side_b_case: Sea-Land Serv., Inc. v. Ceramica Europa II, Inc.
side_b_holding: Void-judgment motions have no time limit.
notes: Petition for cert filed

split_topic: Whether a Heck dismissal counts as a PLRA strike
side_a_holding: Heck dismissal counts as a PLRA strike.
side_b_holding: Heck dismissals do not always count as PLRA strikes.
notes: Good yes/no split; Second Circuit is recent.

split_topic: Whether CWA citizen suits can enforce stricter state permit requirements
side_a_case: Puget Soundkeeper All. v. Port of Tacoma
side_a_holding: Citizen suits may enforce stricter state stormwater permit terms.
side_b_case: Atl. States Legal Found. v. Eastman Kodak
side_b_holding: Citizen suits cannot enforce requirements beyond federal regulations.
notes: Technical CWA split; include for topic diversity.

split_topic: Who bears the burden to prove removal-waiver validity
side_a_holding: Defendant bears burden to prove waiver invalid.
side_b_holding: Government bears burden to prove waiver valid.
notes: Clean burden-of-proof split.

split_topic: Whether FPASA authorizes federal-contractor minimum wage mandates
side_a_holding: FPASA does not authorize federal-contractor minimum wage mandate.
side_b_holding: FPASA permits mandates with economy-efficiency nexus.
notes: Tenth chosen as recent representative.

split_topic: Whether FAA transportation-worker exemption requires transportation-industry employment
side_a_case: Fraga v. Premium Retail Servs., Inc.
side_a_holding: Non-transportation workers may qualify for FAA transportation exemption.
side_b_case: Bissonnette v. LePage Bakeries Park St., LLC
side_b_holding: Transportation-industry employment is necessary for FAA exemption.
notes: Strong FAA split

split_topic: Whether Equal Pay Act defense requires job-related factor other than sex
side_a_case: Eisenhauer v. Culinary Inst. of Am.
side_a_holding: Factor-other-than-sex defense need not be job-related.
side_b_holding: Factor-other-than-sex defense must be job-related.
notes: Side B has multiple circuits; Ninth chosen as recent representative.

split_topic: Whether housing vouchers can be FHA reasonable accommodations
side_a_case: Klossner v. IADU Table Mound MHP, LLC
side_a_holding: Landlords need not accept vouchers as reasonable accommodations.
side_b_holding: Reasonable accommodations may address economic circumstances.
notes: Clear FHA accommodation split; useful civil-rights diversity.

split_topic: Whether habeas petitioners may recover EAJA attorney’s fees
side_a_holding: Habeas petitioners may not recover EAJA fees.
side_b_holding: Habeas petitioners may recover EAJA fees.
notes: Good yes/no fee-recovery split.

split_topic: Whether a traffic citation counts as an intervening arrest
side_a_holding: Traffic citation is not an intervening arrest.
side_b_holding: Traffic stop citation can count as intervening arrest.
notes: Clean Sentencing Guidelines split.

split_topic: Whether terminated asylees may seek adjustment of status
side_a_holding: Terminated asylees cannot seek adjustment under Section 1159(b).
side_b_holding: Asylees need not maintain asylum status to adjust.
notes: Strong yes/no immigration split

split_topic: Whether omitted bankruptcy claims require subjective intent for judicial estoppel
side_a_case: Slater v. United States Steel Corp.
side_a_holding: Courts must consider subjective intent before applying estoppel.
side_b_case: Flugence v. Axis Surplus Ins. Co.
side_b_holding: Motive to conceal can trigger judicial estoppel.
source: Seton Hall Circuit Review, Current Circuit Splits, 2018, p. 283
notes: Tenth also on Side B; Ninth, Seventh, Sixth, Fourth align with Slater.

split_topic: Whether testing a residential door key is a Fourth Amendment search
side_a_holding: Residential key testing was an unreasonable warrantless search.
side_b_holding: Key testing did not require Fourth Amendment suppression.
source: Seton Hall Circuit Review, Current Circuit Splits, 2018, p. 284
notes: Side B also includes Salgado, Thompson, and Moses; Concepcion chosen as anchor.

split_topic: Whether Rule 23(f)’s 14-day deadline can be equitably tolled
side_a_holding: Equitable circumstances may toll Rule 23(f)’s deadline.
side_b_holding: Rule 23(f)’s deadline is strict and inflexible.
source: Seton Hall Circuit Review, Current Circuit Splits, 2018, p. 284
notes: Later reversed by Supreme Court; exclude if unresolved-only set.

split_topic: Whether misprision of felony is categorically a CIMT
side_a_holding: Misprision of felony is not categorically a CIMT.
side_b_holding: Misprision of felony is categorically a CIMT.
source: Seton Hall Circuit Review, Current Circuit Splits, 2018
notes: Second and Ninth align; Fifth and Eleventh take opposite view.

split_topic: Whether Nieves requires comparative evidence for FARA claims
side_a_holding: Comparative evidence required when probable cause supports retaliatory arrest.
side_b_holding: Objective evidence suffices; comparative evidence is not required.
notes: Later addressed by Supreme Court in Gonzalez

split_topic: Whether battering evidence may support a duress defense
side_a_holding: Battering evidence may support duress; physical presence not always required.
side_b_holding: Abuse evidence did not satisfy duress-defense requirements.
notes: Seventh joined Sixth, Ninth, and D.C.; Fifth/Tenth opposite.

split_topic: Whether filing time-barred bankruptcy claims violates the FDCPA
side_a_holding: Time-barred proof of claim may trigger FDCPA liability.
side_b_case: Nelson v. Midland Credit Mgmt., Inc.
side_b_holding: Bankruptcy protections displace FDCPA liability for stale proofs.
notes: Later resolved by Supreme Court in Midland Funding v. Johnson.

split_topic: Whether states have inherent authority to enforce civil immigration law
side_a_holding: States lack inherent authority to enforce civil immigration law.
side_b_holding: State officers may arrest for civil immigration violations.
source: CRS R41423, Authority of State and Local Police to Enforce Federal Immigration Law, pp. 11–14
notes: Sixth aligns with Ninth; Tenth line includes Salinas-Calderon and Vasquez-Alvarez.

split_topic: Whether child-molestation evidence creates probable cause for child-pornography search
side_a_holding: Bare molestation inference alone does not establish probable cause.
side_b_holding: Child-abuse evidence may support child-pornography probable cause.
source: Seton Hall Circuit Review, Current Circuit Splits, 2011
notes: Ninth follows Dougherty; Second, Third, Fourth, Sixth broadly align.

split_topic: Whether Houston mailbox rule governs AEDPA tolling for state postconviction filings
side_a_holding: Mailbox rule applies unless state clearly rejects it.
side_b_holding: State rejection does not bar federal mailbox-rule tolling.
source: Seton Hall Circuit Review, Current Circuit Splits, 2012; Ray v. Clements, 2012
notes: Seventh adopts majority approach; Second uses more federal-law-focused approach.

split_topic: Whether ERISA reimbursement requires tracing specific funds still possessed
side_a_case: Thurber v. Aetna Life Ins. Co.
side_a_holding: Insurer need not trace specific funds still possessed.
side_b_case: Bilyeu v. Morgan Stanley Long Term Disability Plan
side_b_holding: Insurer must identify specific funds still possessed.
source: Seton Hall Circuit Review, Current Circuit Splits, 2013, p. 160
notes: Second aligns with First and Third; Ninth takes stricter tracing approach.

split_topic: When CAFA removal clock starts if damages are unclear
side_a_holding: Clock starts only after clear damages statement or calculable facts.
side_b_holding: Plaintiff must specifically disclose damages amount to trigger clock.
source: Seton Hall Circuit Review, Current Circuit Splits, 2015, p. 384
notes: First adopts Second Circuit approach; split concerns CAFA removal timing under § 1446(b)(3).
